pattern: Fixing the worker with the highest wage-to-quality ratio in the group and keeping the k smallest qualities via a max-heap yields the minimum total payment.
hints: Every paid group has a single effective rate (dollars per quality unit) determined by whoever demands the highest such ratio; every other worker is paid more than their minimum wage at that rate.

Sort workers by their wage/quality ratio. For each candidate as the group's rate-setter, the cheapest k-quality sum is the k smallest qualities seen so far among workers with a ratio no greater than theirs.

Sort by wage[i]/quality[i]. Sweep left to right maintaining a max-heap of quality values of size k; when the heap reaches size k, compute (sum of heap) * ratio[i] and track the minimum. Eject the largest quality whenever the heap exceeds k.

edge_inputs.description: small example: two workers hired, classic case
five workers, hire three, mixed wage/quality ratios
hire all workers — only one valid group exists
k equals 1 so the single cheapest-rate worker wins
a low-quality worker with a high wage still sets the rate for cheaper-ratio workers
equal quality values; rate alone distinguishes cost
all workers have the same wage/quality ratio so any k-group costs the same
high-quality worker with low wage would inflate cost for others when hired together

stress.description: large random workers array with k at half the size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    quality = [rng.randrange(1, 10001) for _ in range(n)]
    wage = [rng.randrange(1, 10001) for _ in range(n)]
    k = max(1, n // 2)
    return [quality, wage, k]
stress.note: k = n//2 so heap pressure is exercised throughout the sweep
